title: nymphaea advena subsp. macrophylla
intro: Nymphaea advena subsp. macrophylla, also known as the large-leaved water lily, is a subspecies of aquatic plant belonging to the family Nymphaeaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Nymphaea advena subsp. macrophylla include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or extract, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and respiratory problems.
